summary: Verified sticky header hysteresis fix in ClientMenuPage. Tested on iPhone SE viewport (390x844) at /demo/1. Hysteresis (mini at >120, full at <40) works — no flicker in 60-100 range, headerMini remains stable when scrollY oscillates below 120.

TEST_1_hysteresis.verdict: PASS - hysteresis works: y=100 stays full going up, stays mini coming down. y=30 (<40) returns to full state (245 ≈ 253, tiny 8px diff is CSS shadow toggle, not header mini state).

TEST_2_flappy_scroll.verdict: PASS - zero re-renders when scrolling rapidly between 60 and 100px (never crosses 120 threshold).

TEST_3_other_features.category_tab_click: PASS - clicking 'Starters' tab scrolls page and highlights the tab (verified via screenshot)
TEST_3_other_features.kitchen_bar_switch: PASS - Bar button clickable, section switches
TEST_3_other_features.anchor_nav: Not deeply verified via ?cat= URL param, but click-based navigation confirmed working

critical_code_review_comments: Fix implementation in /app/frontend/src/pages/ClientMenuPage.jsx lines 28-48 is clean: uses requestAnimationFrame throttling + functional setState with hysteresis thresholds (120px enter mini, 40px exit mini). Correctly avoids re-render loops.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Header sticky hysteresis fix verified working. No regression in category tabs or Kitchen/Bar switch. If retesting anchor navigation, note that cat-tab data-testid pattern was not found — tabs may use different testid; check DOM in ClientMenuPage around line 580-590.
